frictions.description: Browser sessions (agent-browser) were extremely unstable — Escape key, reload, and even fill/type commands would frequently navigate to about:blank, losing all localStorage state and requiring full re-setup. This is a known quirk documented in user-testing.md but far more severe than described.
frictions.resolution: Avoided Escape key entirely, used 'click on other elements' to dismiss dropdowns. Used agent-browser storage commands and JavaScript eval (importTokenlistFile function) to set up state directly instead of relying on page reloads. Used unique session name (grpb) to reduce conflicts with other concurrent subagents.

frictions.description: File upload via agent-browser 'upload' command failed because the Import File button uses a dynamically created hidden file input. Had to use JS function importTokenlistFile() directly instead.
frictions.resolution: Called the app's own importTokenlistFile() function via eval with a synthesized File object, which is functionally equivalent to using the file picker.

frictions.description: Multiple concurrent subagents running agent-browser sessions on the same machine caused resource contention and browser crashes. Browser daemon (PID shared between sessions) would sometimes fail to respond.
frictions.resolution: Used dedicated session name 'grpb', avoided chaining commands, added explicit waits between operations.

summary: Tested 7 assertions: 7 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. All tokenlist management features (import, delete, toggle, export, persistence, XSS prevention) and cross-area autocomplete integration work correctly. Browser instability due to concurrent subagents required workarounds but did not prevent testing.
